title: digitaria divaricatissima
intro: Digitaria divaricatissima, also known as Finger Grass or Finger Millet, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds and infections. The health benefits of Digitaria divaricatissima include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even help to manage diabetes. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine preparations, such as powders and capsules.
